ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, PLAINTIFFS MARTIN GRANADOS & RUTH ANNA GRANADOS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED AS MODIFIED: GRANTED, CONDITIONED ON THE FOLLOWING TERMS THAT ARE JUST PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 473(A): IF WEYERHAEUSER COMPANY ("WC") AND/OR INTERNATIONAL PAPER COMPANY ("IP") SERVE WRITTEN DISCOVERY ON PLAINTIFFS, THE TIME TO RESPOND IS 20 DAYS. WITHIN 3 COURT DAYS OF THIS HEARING, PLAINTIFFS MUST STIPULATE WITH WC AND IP THAT THE THREE PEOPLE ALREADY DEPOSED IN THIS ACTION (MARTIN, RUTH ANNA LOUISE AND JAMES GRANADOS) HAVE NO KNOWLEDGE SUPPORTING PLAINTIFFS' CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES OR, IF THEY WILL NOT SO STIPULATE, MUST PRODUCE THE DEPONENTS FOR FURTHER DEPOSITION NO LATER THAN JUNE 15, 2016, LIMITED TO THE ISSUE OF PUNITIVE DAMAGES. WC AND IP ARE ALLOWED TO FILE MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF PLAINTIFFS' CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES AND PLAINTIFFS MUST STIPULATE TO SHORTENED NOTICE FOR SUCH MOTIONS. THE LAST DAY TO HEAR SUCH MOTIONS, IF THEY ARE FILED, IS JULY 14, 2016 AND THE COURT'S ORDER LIMITING THE CALENDARING OF SUMMARY MOTIONS TO FOUR PER DAY IN THIS CASE DOES NOT APPLY. AS OF THE DATE OF GRANTING PLAINTIFFS' MOTION, THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DEEMED SERVED ON ALL DEFENDANTS WHO HAVE PREVIOUSLY APPEARED. THE RESPECTIVE COUNSEL ARE TO MEET AND CONFER AND AGREE ON THE TIMING OF RESPONSES TO THE PLEADINGS. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFFS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. REPORTER: TARA L. WILLIAMS OF AIKEN WELCH, CSR 13428 (TEL: 510.451.1580)

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT CONTRA COSTA ELECTRIC, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT CONTRA COSTA ELECTRIC, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO SUSTAIN ITS BURDEN OF DEMONSTRATING THAT PLAINTIFFS DO NOT POSSESS AND CANNOT REASONABLY OBTAIN EVIDENCE THAT MR. GRANADOS WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PRODUCTS OR MATERIALS ATTRIBUTABLE TO DEFENDANT. PLAINTIFFS' ARGUMENT THAT THE STIPULATION FOR A SHORTENED NOTICE PERIOD HAS BEEN WITHDRAWN BY THEIR LAWYER IS REJECTED BY THE COURT. NO ENFORCEABLE STIPULATION HAS BEEN SHOWN AND EVEN IF THE EMAIL CORRESPONDENCE IS CONSIDERED AN ENFORCEABLE STIPULATION, IT CONTAINED NO LANGUAGE DEMONSTRATING THE INTENT OF THE PARTIES AS TO WHETHER THE FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH THE TERMS OF THE AGREEMENT WOULD HAVE ANY CONSEQUENCES OR PENALTIES. THEREFORE DEFENDANT'S MOTION IS NOT DENIED ON THE BASIS THAT IT FAILED TO SEND PLAINTIFFS A WORD VERSION OF ITS SEPARATE STATEMENT AT THE TIME IT SERVED ITS MOVING PAPERS. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: GARRETT L. WONG, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, REPORTER: KIMBERLY HENDERSCHOTT, CSR 12552 (AIKEN WELCH, 877.451.1580)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-21-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Christopher B. Bruni of SINUNU BRUNI, LLP (415-362-9700), appearing for defendant / moving party Martin Granados and Ruth Anna Louise Granados
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Western Door & Sash, Co.

RULING:
DEFENDANT WESTERN DOOR & SASH CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Western Door & Sash, Co.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Assuming defendant sustained its initial burden under the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, the deposition testimony and declaration of Mr. Granados, deposition testimony of Mr. Todd and declaration of Mr. Panish create a triable issue whether Mr. Granados was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (fire door mineral cores) attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to the deposition testimony and declaration of Mr. Granados and the deposition testimony of Mr. Todd are overruled. Defendant's objections to the declaration of Mr. Panish are sustained as to paragraphs 9, 10 and 11 and overruled as to the remaining objections. Order signed in open court. Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Reporter: Tara L. Williams of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13428 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/JMQ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-14-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Tiffany Viveros of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Martin Granados and Ruth Ann Louise Granados

RULING:
DEFENDANT T.M. COBB COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant T.M Cobb Company's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Granados was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar because: Mr. Granados testified that Cobb supplied fire doors with mineral cores to J&M; defendant admits that plaintiffs identified James Granados as a product identification witness and defendant failed to demonstrate that James does not have knowledge that Mr. Granados was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant; defendant's Separate Statement did not cite to or rely on plaintiffs' special discovery responses in an attempt to argue they are factually devoid on the issue of asbestos content of any doors that defendant supplied to which Mr. Granados was exposed. Defendant also failed to submit affirmative evidence sufficient to negate an essential element of plaintiffs' claims. Plaintiffs' objections to Mr. Cobb's declaration are sustained as to paragraph 3. Plaintiffs' other objections are moot because no other facts from Mr. Cobb's declaration to which plaintiffs object were contained in defendant's Separate Statement. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Matter not reported. (503/GLW)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Jul-12-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jesse A. Boyd of BUTY & CURLIANO (510-267-3000), appearing for defendant / moving party S.J. Amoroso Construction Co., Inc.
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Martin Granados and Ruth Anna Louise Granados

RULING:
DEFENDANT S.J. AMOROSO CONSTRUCTION CO., INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant S.J. Amoroso Construction Co., Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden. Defendant failed to demonstrate that Mr. Granados did not encounter defendant's employees on any jobsites prior to the late-1970s. Even if Mr. Granados' deposition testimony can be read to conclude that he did not encounter defendant's employees prior to the late-1970s, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that no products or materials to which defendant's employees exposed Mr. Granados contained asbestos. Plaintiffs' objections to defendant's Exhibits E, I and J (manufacturers' interrogatory responses) are sustained. Defendant failed to properly raise the issue of duty in its moving papers. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Reporter: Tara L. Williams of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13428 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/GLW)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-07-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT FDCC CALIFORNIA, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

Appearances: Michael D. Levinson of Brayton Purcell, LLP (415.898.1555) for plaintiff and responding party; Katherine Gardiner of Walsworth (415.781.7072) for defendant and moving party.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant FDCC California, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Defendant bases its arguments on the proposition that the two PG&E sites on Beale Street are the only jobsites where Mr. Granados worked where Dinwiddie employees were present and the deposition testimony and discovery responses demonstrate that plaintiffs do not have evidence to show that Dinwiddie employees exposed him to asbestos at those two sites. Defendant failed to demonstrate, though, that the two PG&E sites are the only sites throughout his career at which Mr. Granados worked where Dinwiddie employees were present. As to defendant's argument related to plaintiffs' response separate statement, the Court considers plaintiffs' opposition papers. Order signed. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Cindy Garis, CSR 9402 (Aiken Welch, 877.451.1580) (503/GLW)

Appearances: Michael D. Levinson of Brayton Purcell (415.898.1555) for plaintiff and responding party; Stephen E. Carlson of Imai Tadlock Keeney & Cordery LLP (415.675.7000) for defendant and moving party.

This matter is not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Frederick Meiswinkel, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs' inability to identify specific jobsites during specific years during which Mr. Granados worked in the presence of defendant's employees, and plaintiffs' inability to identify a brand/manufacturer of joint compound with which they worked, necessarily requires a conclusion that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Granados was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Order signed. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Cindy Garis, CSR 9402 (Aiken Welch, 877.451.1580) (503/GLW)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Jul-06-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT D. ZELINSKY & SONS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

Appearances: Tiffany Viveros of Brayton Purcell LLP (415.898.1555) for plaintiff and responding party; Pamela E. Stevens of Walsworth LLP (415.781.7072) for defendant and moving party.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant D. Zelinsky & Sons, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden. Defendant failed to demonstrate the two propositions upon which it bases its motion. First, it failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs have no evidence regarding when Mr. Granados worked in the presence of defendant's employees. Defense counsel's questioning at deposition and Mr. Granados' responses do not show that Mr. Granados does not know "when the plaintiff claims he was in the presence of D. Zelinsky employees allegedly sanding joint compound." Second, defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs have no evidence regarding the brand name or manufacturer of the joint/taping mud defendant's employees used in the presence of Mr. Granados. On this point, defendant also failed to demonstrate that if plaintiffs could establish that work was prior to 1978, failure to identify a brand/manufacturer of the mud/joint compound is fatal to their claim that it contained asbestos. Order signed. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Tara L. Williams, CSR 13428 (Aiken Welch, 510.451.1580) (503/GLW)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Against Plaintiffs
Off Calendar Jul-05-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT TURNER CONSTRUCTION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

Appearances: Tiffany Viveros of Brayton Purcell LLP (415.898.1555) for plaintiff and responding party; No other appearances.

No notice was received that the ruling would be contested. This matter is not argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Turner Construction Company's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that Mr. Granados was not "exposed to any Turner service, asbestos-containing or otherwise." Defendant only argues proper identification of defendant's employees. Defendant failed to sustain its burden because it did not demonstrate that the UCSF Medical Center job is the only site where plaintiffs and/or their witnesses identified defendant's employees working in the presence of Mr. Granados. For this reason, Willy Mautner's declaration is irrelevant and insufficient to sustain defendant's burden of demonstrating that its employees were not present at any jobsite where Mr. Granados worked. Plaintiffs' argument that the stipulation for a shortened notice period has been withdrawn by their lawyer is rejected by the Court. No enforceable stipulation has been shown and even if the email correspondence is considered an enforceable stipulation, it contained no language demonstrating the intent of the parties as to whether the failure to comply with the terms of the agreement would have any consequences or penalties. Therefore defendant's motion is not denied on the basis that it failed to send plaintiffs a Word version of its Separate Statement at the time it served its moving papers. Order signed. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Erin Robinson, CSR 12199 (Aiken Welch, 877.451.1580) (503/GLW)

MINI MINUTES: INTERVENORS MARYLAND CASUALTY COMPANY, FIREMAN'S FUND INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Intervenors Maryland Casualty Company's and Fireman's Fund Insurance Company's, on behalf of Marconi Plastering, motion for summary judgment is denied. Intervenors failed to sustain their burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Granados was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to Marconi. Intervenors' failure to demonstrate via Mr. Granados' deposition testimony that he has no such knowledge and plaintiffs' responses to intervenors' special interrogatories preclude intervenors from sustaining their burden under the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Specifically, while intervenors addressed the UCSF site, they failed to demonstrate that the UCSF site was the only time Mr. Granados worked in the presence of Marconi employees during his career. Plaintiffs' argument that the stipulation for a shortened notice period has been withdrawn by their lawyer is rejected by the Court. No enforceable stipulation has been shown and even if the email correspondence is considered an enforceable stipulation, it contained no language demonstrating the intent of the parties as to whether the failure to comply with the terms of the agreement would have any consequences or penalties. Therefore intervenors' motion is not denied on the basis that they failed to send plaintiffs a Word version of its Separate Statement at the time it served its moving papers. Order signed. MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CUPERTINO ELECTRIC COMPANY, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

Appearances: Michael D. Levinson of Brayton Purcell (415.898.1555) for plaintiff and responding party; No other appearances.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Cupertino Electric Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Granados was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant's failure to demonstrate via Mr. Granados' deposition testimony that he has no such knowledge and plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from sustaining its burden under the factually devoid prong. Defendant also failed to negate an essential element of plaintiffs' claims. When read in conjunction with Mr. Granados' deposition testimony, the declaration of James Ryley merely creates a triable issue of material fact whether defendant's employees worked at the same job sites as plaintiff. Plaintiffs' argument that the stipulation for a shortened notice period has been withdrawn by their lawyer is rejected by the Court. No enforceable stipulation has been shown and even if the email correspondence is considered an enforceable stipulation, it contained no language demonstrating the intent of the parties as to whether the failure to comply with the terms of the agreement would have any consequences or penalties. Therefore defendant's motion is not denied on the basis that it failed to send plaintiffs a Word version of its Separate Statement at the time it served its moving papers. Order signed. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported

Andrew Chew of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties Martin Granados and Ruth Anna Granados
Paul Lecky of BECHERER KANNETT & SCHWEITZER (510-658-3600), appearing for defendant / opposing party International Paper Company
Andrew I. Port of SEDGWICK LLP (415-781-7900), appearing for defendant / opposing party Weyerhaeuser Company

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS MARTIN GRANADOS & RUTH ANNA GRANADOS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as modified: Plaintiffs' motion for leave to file first amended complaint is granted, conditioned on the following terms that are just pursuant to C.C.P. 473(a): If Weyerhaeuser Company ("WC") and/or International Paper Company ("IP") serve written discovery on plaintiffs, the time to respond is 20 days. Within 3 court days of this hearing, plaintiffs must stipulate with WC and IP that the three people already deposed in this action (Martin, Ruth Anna Louise and James Granados) have no knowledge supporting plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages or, if they will not so stipulate, must produce the deponents for further deposition no later than June 15, 2016, limited to the issue of punitive damages. WC and IP are allowed to file motions for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages and plaintiffs must stipulate to shortened notice for such motions. The last day to hear such motions, if they are filed, is July 14, 2016 and the Court's order limiting the calendaring of summary motions to four per day in this case does not apply. As of the date of granting plaintiffs' motion, the First Amended Complaint is deemed served on all defendants who have previously appeared. The respective counsel are to meet and confer and agree on the timing of responses to the pleadings. Counsel for plaintiffs to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Reporter: Tara L. Williams of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13428 (Tel: 510-451-1580)

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties Martin Granados and Ruth Granados
Laura Przetak of SPANOS | PRZETAK (510-250-0200), Designated Defense Counsel

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF
Tentative amended: Plaintiffs' motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a) & (d). Trial date is July 25, 2016 at 11:15am in Department 503. 120th day is July 28, 2016. The Trial Case Management Order dated May 7, 2014 and the Informal Discovery Conference Order dated July 17, 2015, now included in Local Rule 20, shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is July 19, 2016. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than June 3, 2016. Responses to the demand are due no later than June 17, 2016. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Pang Ly by July 18, 2016 at 2:00p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 at 9:30a.m. on July 21, 2016. Fact discovery cut-off date is July 8, 2016. Expert discovery cut-off date is July 22, 2016. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than April 15, 2016. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents. No later than April 6, 2016 close of business plaintiffs must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims. No later than April 8, 2016 close of business defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on April 27, 2016 at 1:30p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the Court pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court Rule 5 on July 25, 2016 at 1:30p.m. in Department 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rule 5.0 and provide a confidential settlement statement by July 21, 2016 at 2:00p.m.. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Reporter: Michele Lucas of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #4017 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/GLW)
